items.title: Songs are reaching 1bn Spotify streams 14x faster than a decade ago, and other stats from Chartmetric's 2025 Year in Music report
items.author: James Hanley
items.verbatim_quote: draws on the platform's data from millions of tracks and artist profiles collected since 2016... spans nine chapters covering everything from artist development and genre trends to streaming dynamics, sync licensing, songwriting, and the shifting geography of global hits
items.why_relevant: Confirms Chartmetric's catalog-scale data scope (tracks + artists + sync licensing + genre + geography) Sharon's dashboard should mirror in its catalog views.

items.title: AI Music Deals, a Rock Comeback and Country's Coastal Invasion: Trends That Defined the Music Business in 2025
items.author: Ethan Millman, Nicole Fell
items.verbatim_quote: The labels need access to indie artists. It's all about the users. They need their users. No one wants to be in the major label system. The majors are going to evolve more toward services companies.
items.why_relevant: Frames the 2025 shift toward labels-as-services and indie catalog access — the exact whitespace Sharon's catalog dashboard targets for independent rightsholders.

items.title: The 23 biggest music business deals of 2025: From Taylor Swift to Tencent Music, Live Nation, and Chord
items.author: Daniel Tencer
items.verbatim_quote: Asset-backed securitization (ABS) of music catalogs has truly taken off
items.why_relevant: Catalog ABS replacing outright catalog buyouts as the dominant deal type means Sharon's dashboard must surface securitization-grade catalog metrics (royalty stability, valuation, payout history), not just streaming counts.
